title: $formdb (deprecated)
text: $formdb (deprecated) Return a value that indicates whether data in the current Form has been retrieved from a database. Note: The $formdb function is deprecated. It has been superseded by $instancedb , which is applicable to all component types. For more information, see $instancedb . Return = $formdb Return Values Table 1. Values returned in $formdb Value Meaning 0 In the following cases: No entities have been retrieved from a database. $formdb has been reset to 0 by a ProcScript statement. No entities are painted on the form. 1 An entity in the form has been retrieved from a database. Use Use in Form components. Description Use $formdb to test whether any occurrence in the Form has been retrieved from a database. The value of $formdb is actually the inclusive logical OR of all the entity-level flags indicating database origin. Statements that affect the value of $formdb are the same as those that affect $instancedb , and the return values are determined in the same way. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
